verbGlossary.ASSEMBLED BY: {source} compiles compliance letters, inspection reports, and supporting documentation into a complete regulatory closeout package.
verbGlossary.ATTENDED BY: {source} is physically present during a regulatory or AHJ inspection to answer technical questions and provide access to work areas.
verbGlossary.AUDITED BY: {source} reviews compliance document sets for completeness and accuracy, identifying missing permits or expired certifications before regulatory deadlines.
verbGlossary.AUTHORED BY: {source} writes and maintains a regulatory plan (e.g., site safety plan) incorporating OSHA standards, site-specific hazards, and emergency procedures.
verbGlossary.AUTHORIZED BY: {source} grants owner-level approval for a permit application to proceed, confirming the project entity has standing to request {target}.
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED BY: {source} compares an inspection or permit metric against project targets and portfolio norms to gauge regulatory performance.
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONED BY: {source} engages and oversees a third-party testing agency, defining test scope, witnessing procedures, and accepting results.
verbGlossary.COORDINATED BY: {source} aligns regulatory inspection timing, permit sequencing, or agency submittals with the construction schedule and field readiness.
verbGlossary.ENFORCED BY: {source} ensures subcontractors and trades comply with prevailing wage, certified payroll, and labor regulation requirements throughout the project.
verbGlossary.EXPEDITED BY: {source} accelerates {target} or certificate through the approval process by resolving reviewer comments, resubmitting corrections, and maintaining agency contact.
verbGlossary.FACILITATED BY: {source} manages the interface between the project team and a regulatory agency, routing submittals, scheduling reviews, and resolving conditions of approval.
verbGlossary.FILED BY: {source} submits a required regulatory notification or report to the governing agency within mandated timeframes.
verbGlossary.MAINTAINED BY: {source} keeps {target} in active compliance through ongoing field practices, monitoring, and periodic reporting (e.g., SWPPP inspections and logs).
verbGlossary.MONITORED BY: {source} tracks permit status, expiration dates, or regulatory metrics over time, flagging items that require action before they delay the project.
verbGlossary.OBTAINED BY: {source} secures {target} or regulatory approval by completing applications, coordinating reviews, and satisfying conditions of issuance.
verbGlossary.PETITIONED BY: {source} prepares and presents a formal request for a zoning variance or special exception before a regulatory board or hearing body.
verbGlossary.PREPARED BY: {target} creates {source} application or regulatory submission, assembling drawings, forms, fees, and supporting documentation.
verbGlossary.PULLED BY: {source} physically obtains a construction permit from the issuing jurisdiction, often requiring in-person pickup or portal download after approval.
verbGlossary.QUANTIFIED BY: {source} measures the schedule and cost impact of a regulatory hold, translating days of delay into float consumption and potential liquidated damages.
verbGlossary.REQUESTED BY: {source} initiates {target} application or utility connection request on behalf of the project, providing required project data to the issuing authority.
verbGlossary.REVIEWED BY: {source} examines {target} application or regulatory submittal for technical accuracy, code compliance, and completeness before submission to the agency.
verbGlossary.SCHEDULED BY: {source} arranges the date and time for a regulatory inspection, coordinating between the AHJ and field crews to ensure work is ready for review.
verbGlossary.SECURED BY: {source} obtains a zoning or land-use approval during preconstruction by navigating municipal review processes, public hearings, and conditional requirements.
verbGlossary.SHEPHERDED BY: {source} guides a plan review submission through the building department review cycle, tracking reviewer comments and coordinating resubmittals across disciplines.
verbGlossary.SUBMITTED BY: {target} files {source} application or regulatory document with the issuing authority, ensuring all required forms and attachments are included.
verbGlossary.TRACKED BY: {source} maintains a current log of permit statuses, inspection results, and regulatory milestones, keeping the project team informed of upcoming deadlines.
verbGlossary.VERIFIED BY: {source} confirms that all systems and conditions required for a certificate have been tested, documented, and meet the standards of the issuing authority.
verbGlossary.WITNESSED BY: {source} observes a special inspection or test in person, confirming that procedures follow the approved testing plan and documenting results for the record.
